query: PyeongChang Loppet cross country ski race Alpensia Cross-Country Centre

videos.title: Sweden’s Charlotte Kalla, Norway’s Marit Bjoergen take gold, silver in women's skiathon
videos.description: The first silver medalist of the 2018 Winter Olympics hugged the first gold medalist with a heartfelt gusto.The first bronze medalist expressed pleasant surprise.The gold medalist felt happy for the fourth-place finisher who is, after all, just 20.In came the fifth-place finisher, the Minnesotan who crossed the line and sprawled to the cold, hard ground with both cramps and a bolstered sense of hope.That silver medalist had made Olympic history, not that she mulled that.All told, a fine delirium swirled around the finish line at the Alpensia Cross-Country Centre on a cold Korean Saturday afternoon.The PyeongChang Olympics had begun handing out medals.For the women’s 15-kilometer cross-country skiathlon, with its first half in the classical style and its second half in the freestyle and its usual inhumane upslopes for both styles, Sweden’s Charlotte Kalla, 30, fulfilled her wish to upgrade from the silver medal she got in Sochi in 2014.Finland’s 27-year-old Krista Parmakoksi got bronze and said, “Now I make it.”That fifth-place finisher, the effusive Jessie Diggins from a country [United States] yet to win an Olympic women’s cross-country medal, hit the ground 4.6seconds from a medal and said, “Being seconds out of a medal is so exciting because I know it’s possible.I have the belief.I have the confidence.” [Jessie Diggins is ‘sparkly.’She also is poised to make history.] Amid them all, there was that silver medalist, the one who finished 7.8seconds behind Kalla, and the one who got stuck briefly behind another racer when Kalla broke compellingly from the pack around 12.5kilometers.The silver minted Marit Bjoergen, the Norwegian who will reach age 38 in March, as the world’s most decorated female Winter Olympian.It became her 11th medal across five Olympics: a silver in Salt Lake City in 2002; a silver in Turin, Italy, in 2006; three golds and a silver and a bronze in Vancouver in 2010; three golds in Sochi, Russia, in 2014, and this.Yet this was also medal No.1 in another role, that of mother, one she has held down for two years and change.While she seemed unmoved with that former distinction about the 11 medals and whatnot, saying she’d just see “how many medals I have” once she gets home, the latter did matter.“Things are different after I’ve been a mom,” she said.“Things are more important to me, to be a good mom and then to do a good race.So to still be there and be fighting for medals is, I’m really happy with that.” Asked if silver suited her as she tried to become the first cross-country skier to win three straight golds in an individual event, she said, “Yeah, I’m really happy.” Asked if she felt disappointed, she said, “No, I’m not disappointed.” And as an Olympics emphasizing friendship got underway, she emphasized friendship, for a fresh gold medalist with whom Bjoergen has trained.“For me, Charlotte is a very good friend for me also, so I’m really happy for her today,” Bjoergen said.“I feel like she’s been the strongest this year.

videos.title: 2013 Gatineau Loppet 55k Classic Race Review
videos.description:
ActiveSteve, not even recovered from tackling the Canadian Ski Marathon the week before, heads back out with his skis on to cover another classic Canadian ski event, the Gatineau Loppet, heading into it's 35th year. To commemorate the 35th year, the Classic event featured a 55km and 38km linear (point-to-point) route instead of the normal looped course. Under brilliant skies and great snow conditions, he lined up with participants from around the world. Check out the video to see how is all unrolled.

Music: Dan-O Songs

videos.title: Skadi Loppet Teaser 2020
videos.channel: Euroloppet Your World of Ski Marathon
videos.description: Skadi Loppet is a Ski Marathon Race of Euroloppet, the most famous Ski Marathon Series in Europe. Skadi Loppet exists since 1990 and celebrates its 30th anniversary in 2020. The race is scheduled on the thrid weekend of March in Bodenmais/ Germany have only been canceled once in 30 years.
